response:
The equilibrium population size occurs when the rate of change is zero so we set the derivative to zero and solve for P. With r equal to 0.1 and K equal to 1000 and a constant average harvest of 10 since the sine term averages to zero over a full cycle we find P equals 900 turtles as the steady state value where growth balances loss.

When the carrying capacity decreases linearly over time the new equation becomes dP over dt equals 0.1 times P times 1 minus P divided by 1000 minus 3t minus 10 minus 5 sine of pi t over 6. To find the time when the population is no longer sustainable we look for when the positive equilibrium root becomes zero or negative. The equilibrium condition simplifies to r times P times 1 minus P over K equals the average harvest of 10. Solving the quadratic equation for P gives us the positive root which exists only if the discriminant is nonnegative. This requires r times K to be greater than or equal to 4 times the harvest divided by r. Substituting the values we get 0.1 times 1000 minus 3t must be at least 400. This inequality simplifies to 100 minus 0.3t being greater than or equal to 40. Solving for t yields t less than or equal to 200 years. At t equals 200 the carrying capacity drops to 400 and the maximum sustainable yield matches the harvest rate exactly. Beyond this point the population collapses to zero because the environment cannot support the required growth to offset the constant losses. The system loses stability as the equilibrium point vanishes and the only remaining state is extinction. A palindrome that fits this context is level.
